title: Rich Smoky French Beef Fillet Quiche
description: A silky, high-protein quiche with seared beef fillet, smoky paprika, and a deglaze fond of red wine, perfect for a weeknight French dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C and roll out puff pastry onto a 9-inch pie dish, trimming excess.
In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp oil over medium-high, sear beef slices until browned, remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add remaining oil, onion, and garlic; sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in smoked paprika, cook 1 minute, then deglaze with red wine, scraping up the fond and reducing by half.
Spread the onion mixture over the pastry, then arrange the seared beef on top.
In a bowl, whisk eggs, heavy cream, salt, and pepper until well blended.
Pour the custard over the beef, then sprinkle grated gruyere evenly on top.
Bake for 35 minutes until the custard is set and the top is golden brown.
Remove from oven and let the quiche cool for 10 minutes before slicing.
Garnish with fresh thyme sprigs and serve warm.
